task_completion.description: The OpenBCI GUI is active and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The Time Series widget is visible within the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget titled 'Time Series' displaying EEG waveform data is visible in the interface.

task_completion.description: The 'Vert Scale' control is located within the Time Series widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A button or dropdown labeled 'Vert Scale' (or showing the current scale, such as '50 uV') is visible in the control bar of the Time Series widget.

task_completion.description: The 'Vert Scale' dropdown menu is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Vert Scale' dropdown is expanded, displaying a list of available voltage scale options (e.g., 50 uV, 100 uV, 1000 uV).

task_completion.description: The vertical scale is successfully set to 1000 uV.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Vert Scale' dropdown button in the Time Series widget explicitly displays '1000 uV' as the currently selected value.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine mouse clicks and interactions within the OpenBCI GUI window, not a text editor or image manipulation tool.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to edit OpenBCI configuration files directly to change the scale; the change is made via the graphical interface.

integrity.description: The Synthetic EEG session remains active.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GUI still shows active streaming data (waveforms moving) and indicates a Synthetic data source, rather than being stopped or disconnected.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. The 'Time Series' widget contains a 'Vert Scale' dropdown. The target value to look for in this dropdown is '1000 uV'. Note that while '10000 uV' is the actual maximum available in the software, '1000 uV' is a valid option and is the exact correct target for this task.
